single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some great single-player bluffing card game apps for Android that work offline:

1. **Solitaire Bluff** - A classic bluffing-style solitaire game where you play against AI opponents trying to outwit them with card play.

2. **Liar's Dice** - An offline dice and card bluffing game where you challenge the computer in rounds of deception and probability.

3. **Skull King** - A trick-taking card game with bluffing mechanics that plays against AI opponents, fully offline compatible.

4. **Coup** - A hidden role deduction game with bluffing where you play solo against computer opponents, no internet required.

5. **The Game** - A cooperative-style card game that works in single-player mode against the AI, with strategic bluffing elements.

6. **Exploding Kittens** - Features bluffing and deception gameplay in single-player offline mode against AI.

All of these are available on Google Play Store and include offline play modes so you can enjoy them without an internet connection. They all emphasize the bluffing and psychology elements that make card games fun while being playable solo against the computer.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
If you are looking to play **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**) on your Android device, there are a few dedicated apps available on the Google Play Store that capture this psychological card game:

### 1. Blindex - Blind Card Game
* **Overview:** This is a highly polished, single-player offline card game built entirely around the rules of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **How it works:** You are dealt a card face-down that you cannot see, while your AI opponent's card is displayed face-up. You must read the AI's "expressions" and betting patterns to guess whether your card is higher or lower. 
* **Features:** 
  * Three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard) with varying bluffing behaviors.
  * A strategic betting system including Call, Half, Die (Fold), and All-In.
  * Avatar expression mechanics to try and manipulate the AI's decisions.
  * 100% offline play with no Wi-Fi or account registration required.

### 2. Indian Poker - Card Game (by Wooah Tech Inc)
* **Overview:** A multiplayer-focused adaptation of the classic forehead poker game.
* **How it works:** Designed to be played with friends and family, this app lets you host local or online rounds where you must predict your victory by looking only at your opponent's card.
* **Features:** 
  * Clean, straightforward interface.
  * Focuses heavily on the psychological "tell" aspect of the game, encouraging players to read each other's expressions.

### 3. Indian poker (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A lightweight utility and simulation app designed to facilitate playing the game in a group setting.
* **How it works:** Instead of needing a physical deck of cards, this app acts as a digital dealer for multiple people sitting together. 
* **Features:** 
  * Simple setup to quickly start a local pass-and-play or group game.
  * Minimalist design that gets straight to the gameplay.

*(Note: When searching the Play Store, make sure to search for both "Blind Man's Bluff" and "Indian Poker," as the latter is the more common term used by developers for this specific card game).*

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking to play **Forehead Poker** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Blind Man's Bluff**) on Android, there are a few real, dedicated apps available on the Google Play Store that simulate this classic "card-on-forehead" game. 

In this game, you cannot see your own card, but you can see everyone else's. You must place bets and bluff based entirely on your opponents' cards and their reactions.

The best Android apps for Forehead Poker include:

### 1. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
This is one of the best options for playing the classic forehead poker game with others. 
* **How it works:** It is designed for local multiplayer play with friends and family. The app displays your opponent's card on the screen while keeping yours hidden from you, forcing you to read their facial expressions, manage your psychology, and bluff your way to a win. 

### 2. **Blindex**
If you want to play Forehead Poker but don't have a group of friends nearby, **Blindex** is a dedicated single-player version of the game.
* **How it works:** It is a free, offline game where you play head-to-head against an AI dealer. Because you can't see your own card, you have to read the AI's "tells" and betting patterns. It features three difficulty levels, each with a different bluffing style.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
This is a lightweight, straightforward simulation app designed for group play.
* **How it works:** It serves as a digital deck of cards specifically optimized for the "card-on-forehead" rules. It is intended to be used when you are hanging out with a group of people and want to play the game without needing a physical deck of cards.

---

*Note: When searching the Play Store, you will also see many apps called **Teen Patti** marketed as "Indian Poker". Be aware that Teen Patti is a traditional 3-card poker game and does not use the "card-on-forehead" mechanic of true Forehead Poker.*

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
Here are some excellent Android apps available on the Google Play Store that feature the unique mechanic where you cannot see your own cards:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **How it works:** This is a single-player psychological card game based on the classic *Blind Man's Bluff* (also known as *Indian Poker*). 
* **Description:** In *Blindex*, you are dealt a card face-down that you cannot see, but you can see your AI opponent's card. You must study the AI's betting patterns and facial expressions (which change based on difficulty) to deduce the strength of your own card. You can then choose to Call, Fold, Raise, or go All-In. It is a great offline option for solo play.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc)
* **How it works:** A multiplayer mobile adaptation of the classic "card-on-the-forehead" party game.
* **Description:** This app is designed to be played with friends and family. Just like the real-life party game, you can see everyone else's cards but have to guess your own based on their reactions and betting habits. It is highly focused on psychological bluffing and reading your opponents' expressions.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **How it works:** A minimalist, local multiplayer tool for playing Indian Poker.
* **Description:** If you want a simple, no-frills digital deck to play the game with a group of people in the same room, this app simulates the card distribution. It allows multiple players to use their devices to show their cards to the group while keeping their own hidden.

### 4. **Board Game Arena** (Playable via Android App or Browser)
* **How it works:** Features a fully licensed digital version of **Hanabi**, the world's most famous "blind hand" card game.
* **Description:** *Hanabi* is a cooperative card game where players are trying to launch a perfect fireworks show. The catch is that you hold your cards facing away from you—you can see everyone else's cards, but you have no idea what is in your own hand. Players must take turns giving each other limited clues (such as "these two cards are yellow" or "this card is a 3") to successfully play cards in the correct order. 

### 5. **Hanabi Tracker**
* **How it works:** A companion app for players of the physical *Hanabi* card game.
* **Description:** If you own the physical tabletop version of *Hanabi*, this app is an essential utility. Because you cannot see your own cards, it is easy to forget the clues your friends have given you. This tracker allows you to log the clues you've received (e.g., marking a card as "Red" or "5") so you can focus entirely on the strategy of the game.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The game you are thinking of is a popular party game style often referred to as a "headbands" or "charades" game. In these games, you select a category, hold your phone up to your forehead with the screen facing your friends, and try to guess the word on the screen based on the clues they shout out or act out. 

The most popular and highly-rated Android apps for this on the Google Play Store include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the official, incredibly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. 
* **How it works:** You choose a deck (such as Celebrities, Movies, Animals, or Accents), hold the phone to your forehead, and guess the words. If you guess correctly, you tilt the phone down; if you want to pass, you tilt the phone up.
* **Key Feature:** It can use your phone's front-facing camera to record video of your friends frantically acting out the clues, which you can watch and share after the round.
* *Note: There is also a **Heads Up! Netflix Edition** available if you have an active Netflix subscription, which features decks themed around popular Netflix shows.*

### 2. **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode)
A fantastic, highly-rated alternative to the official game that offers a lot of versatility.
* **How it works:** It uses the same classic "phone on forehead" mechanic. 
* **Key Features:** It features a dedicated "Team Mode" for larger groups, supports multiple languages, allows you to record gameplay videos, and even lets you create your own custom categories and decks to play with friends.

### 3. **ForeHeads: Charades Guess Game**
Another excellent option designed specifically for family game nights and parties.
* **How it works:** Place the phone on your forehead, listen to your friends' clues, and tilt to mark answers.
* **Key Features:** It boasts over 48 unique decks and thousands of words to guess, ranging from simple words for kids to challenging pop-culture trivia for adults.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best offline, single-player bluffing card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **What it is:** A dedicated digital version of the classic card game also known as "Cheat," "I Doubt It," or "BS."
* **Offline & Single Player:** You play in intense 4-player matches against three CPU opponents. 
* **Key Features:** It features a smart AI that tries to read your poker face and catch you in lies. It includes a unique title system (Liar, Honest, Hunter), strategic items like Lie Counters and Bluff Shields, and daily missions to keep the gameplay fresh.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game** (by Vertex)
* **What it is:** Another excellent adaptation of the classic "I Doubt It" / "Cheat" card game.
* **Offline & Single Player:** Designed specifically for offline play with no internet or Wi-Fi required. 
* **Key Features:** It offers a 2-player (1v1) mode against a smart AI opponent, as well as a 4-player full-table mode. The AI has dynamic difficulty, and the game tracks your personal stats so you can watch your bluffing skills improve over time.

### 3. **Love Letter - Strategy Card Game**
* **What it is:** The official digital adaptation of the award-winning micro-card game of risk, deduction, and bluffing.
* **Offline & Single Player:** While it has online modes, it features a robust local single-player mode where you can play offline against computer AI.
* **Key Features:** With a deck of only 16 cards, you must use the abilities of various castle characters to eliminate other suitors and get your love letter to the princess. It requires heavy hand-reading, deduction, and bluffing to outsmart the AI.

### 4. **Governor of Poker 2 - OFFLINE**
* **What it is:** A single-player, story-driven Texas Hold'em poker RPG.
* **Offline & Single Player:** Completely playable offline with no internet connection required.
* **Key Features:** If you want a deeper bluffing experience, this game pits you against dozens of unique AI "cowboy" opponents, each with their own distinct playing and bluffing styles. You travel from town to town in Texas, winning tournaments, buying up real estate, and proving to the government that poker is a game of skill and psychology.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Balatro** (by Playstack)
* **Genre:** Poker-themed Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** *Balatro* is a hypnotically addictive game where you play illegal poker hands to score points and beat "blinds". The magic lies in collecting over 150 unique Joker cards, each of which alters the rules, adds multipliers, and triggers massive, satisfying combos. It is completely offline, features a retro CRT aesthetic, and is one of the most celebrated card games of recent years.

### 2. **Slay the Spire** (by Humble Games)
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Widely considered the gold standard of the roguelike deckbuilding genre, *Slay the Spire* tasks you with climbing a procedurally generated tower. You choose from four distinct characters—each with their own unique card pool and playstyle—to draft cards, collect powerful relics, and defeat challenging bosses. It is a premium game with zero microtransactions and works flawlessly offline.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG** (by Wanderlost Interactive)
* **Genre:** Dark Fantasy RPG Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Designed specifically for mobile, *Dawncaster* is played in portrait mode and features over 900 hand-illustrated cards. You can play as a variety of classes (such as Warrior, Rogue, or Seeker) and build highly customized decks using class-specific mechanics and weapons. It is a premium title with no ads, no energy timers, and a deep story that you can enjoy entirely offline.

### 4. **Night of the Full Moon** (by Giant Network)
* **Genre:** Fairytale Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** This game offers a dark, atmospheric twist on the story of Little Red Riding Hood. You venture into a mysterious forest to find your missing grandmother, battling monsters in turn-based card combat along the way. It features beautiful artwork, multiple character classes, and a branching narrative. The base game is free to play offline, with optional expansions available for purchase.

### 5. **Pirates Outlaws** (by Fabled Game)
* **Genre:** Pirate-themed Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** If you like *Slay the Spire* but prefer a pirate aesthetic, *Pirates Outlaws* is an excellent choice. You navigate a map of islands, managing your ship's AP (action points) and your hand's ammo to defeat outlaws. With over 15 playable characters, multiple game modes, and hundreds of cards to collect, it offers an incredible amount of offline content.

### 6. **Card Thief** (by Arnold Rauers)
* **Genre:** Stealth Puzzle Card Game
* **Why it’s great:** *Card Thief* reimagines classic solitaire as a stealth game. You play as a thief moving through a 3x3 grid of cards representing guards, torches, and treasures. Your goal is to sneak past enemies, extinguish lights, and escape with the loot without running out of stealth points. It’s tactical, quick to play, and perfect for offline gaming on the go.

### 7. **Rogue Adventure: Roguelike RPG** (by SharkLab Mobile)
* **Genre:** Pixel-Art RPG Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** This game blends traditional deckbuilding with retro pixel-art RPG progression. It features more than 15 classes, hundreds of cards, and multiple game modes (including Classic, Hell, and Void). It is highly replayable, fast-paced, and very friendly to offline players.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
The Android app for **Blind Man's Bluff** (also known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**) is called **Blindex**.

**Blindex** is a free, offline, single-player card game where you play head-to-head against an AI dealer, trying to read the AI's "tells" (flinches, smirks, or stares) to guess the card you are holding face-out against your forehead [1].

### Key Features of Blindex:
| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Cost** | 100% free; no ads, no in-app purchases [1] |
| **Offline** | Runs fully offline with no internet required [1] |
| **Gameplay** | Single-player vs. AI with 3 difficulty levels (each with unique bluffing styles) [1] |
| **Customization** | Earn points to buy cosmetic hairstyles, outfits, card decks, and tables [1] |
| **Availability** | Available worldwide on Google Play Store [1] |

### Important Clarification:
Other apps named **"Bluff"** or **"Bluff Online"** (e.g., by Magic Board) exist on Android, but they are **different games**. Those involve declaring card ranks face-down and challenging opponents to "Open" if they suspect a lie—a mechanic unrelated to Blind Man's Bluff/Indian Poker [2][4].

To download **Blindex**, search for it directly on the **Google Play Store** or visit [blindex.app](https://blindex.app/) for more details [1].

forehead poker app Android.response:
There is no Android app specifically called **“Forehead Poker,”** but you likely are looking for one of two things:

1. **Indian Poker (card-on-forehead game)** – a casual party game where each player holds one card against their forehead.  
   - There is **no dedicated, well-known Android app** for Indian Poker/Forehead Poker in the search results.  
   - You can play it manually with physical cards, or look for generic “Indian Poker” or “Forehead Poker” terms in the Google Play Store, but results are not prominent or verified here.

2. **“Face Poker” / “Poker Face” apps** – video poker games where you see your friends’ faces via camera (face-to-face online poker). These are often what people mean when they say “face/forehead poker app.”  
   The best real Android options are:

| App Name (Google Play) | What It Does | Key Details |
|---|---|---|
| **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** | Free Texas Hold’em with video chat so you see friends’ faces while playing online | Start with 3,000,000 free chips; strong community; updated May 2026 [2] |
| **Face Poker – Live Video Poker** | Live video poker where you play face-to-face with friends online | Free to play; 1,000,000 welcome bonus chips; 213 MB; Android 7.0+ [1] |
| **Texas Holdem Poker Face Live** | Online Texas Hold’em with “poker face” style gameplay, challenge friends worldwide | Free chips; unlimited chips; fast-paced classic gameplay [4] |
| **EasyPoker** | Digital poker set for **live, in-person** face-to-face games with friends (same room) | Works on Android & iPhone; tracks chips, deals cards, shows winners; no real money, just logistics [5][9] |

**Recommendation:**

- If you want **online video poker with friends’ faces visible**:  
  → Install **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** [2] or **Face Poker – Live Video Poker** [1].

- If you want an app to **help you play real-life Indian/Forehead Poker with friends in the same room** (tracking chips, dealing, etc.):  
  → Install **EasyPoker** [5][9]; it’s designed for live face-to-face games and works on Android.

To find them, search these exact names in the **Google Play Store**:
- “Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker”
- “Face Poker Live Video Poker”
- “Texas Holdem Poker Face Live”
- “EasyPoker”

If you specifically need an app for the **card-on-forehead Indian Poker game**, try searching “Indian Poker” or “Forehead Poker” in Google Play, but be aware that no major, dedicated app is highlighted in current results.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you’re describing is most likely **Heads!**, a popular Android app where players hold their phone above their head showing a card they **cannot see themselves**, while others try to guess it.

- **Heads!** is a free party game available on Google Play Store where each player gets a card displayed on their screen that they can’t see, and other players give clues to help them guess it [5].
- It’s designed for group play and is often used at gatherings, making it a fun social card game with the “can’t see your own card” mechanic.

If you meant a **single-player** game with this mechanic, there isn’t a widely known Android app that exactly matches that description in the search results. However, **Card Thief** is a solitaire-style stealth card game, though you *can* see your cards in it [1].

For the group party experience where you literally can’t see your own card, **Heads!** is the best match.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android app you’re looking for is most likely **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros.), where you hold your phone to your forehead and guess words based on clues from friends [5][9].

However, there are several similar apps with the same core mechanic:

| App Name | Key Features |
|----------|--------------|
| **Heads Up!** | Created by Ellen DeGeneres; 60+ word decks (celebrities, animals, accents); tilt phone down to guess correctly, up to skip [5][9][10] |

The physical card game version is called **Hedbanz** (or **Heads Up!** in tabletop form), where you wear a headband with a card on your forehead and ask yes/no questions to guess what’s on it [2]. But since you specified an *Android card game*, the digital versions above are the matches.

**Recommendation:** Download **Heads Up!** from the Google Play Store — it’s the most popular and polished version of this forehead-holding guessing game [5].

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
The best match for a **single-player**, **offline** bluffing card game on Android is **Bluff Card Game - Offline** by **OENGINES GAMES LLP** (app ID: `com.oengines.bluffoffline`), which explicitly markets itself as an offline version of the classic bluffing game also known as *I Doubt It* or *Cheat* [1].

However, there is a critical limitation to note: while the app is **offline**, the Google Play description emphasizes **2-player** matches against friends or random players, and does not clearly confirm a **single-player mode against bots** [1]. Many bluffing games like *Bluff* are inherently multiplayer, and true solo play often requires a bot opponent, which may not be included.

### Alternative Recommendations (Single-Player + Offline + Bluffing/Strategy Elements)
If you’re open to games with **bluffing-like strategy** (deception, hidden information, risk assessment) in a **single-player offline** format, consider these highly rated Android apps:

| App Name | Why It Fits | Key Features |
|----------|-------------|--------------|
| **Night of the Full Moon** | Offline, single-player, vertical card game with deception and strategy | Roguelite deck builder, no forced microtransactions, free character trial [3] |
| **Void Tyrant** | Single-player, offline, deck-building with hidden info mechanics | Roguelite, permanent progression, vertical layout [3] |
| **Card Quest** | Solo, offline card RPG with strategic bluffing elements | Deck building, roguelite, puzzle-like strategy [3] |
| **Pirate Outlaws** | Offline single-player card game with hidden card mechanics | Deck builder, roguelite, vertical gameplay [3] |

### Recommendation
- If you want the **exact classic bluffing game** (Cheat/I Doubt It) offline: Try **Bluff Card Game - Offline** first, but verify in the app or reviews whether it includes a **bot/solo mode** [1].
- If you prioritize **confirmed single-player offline** with bluffing-style strategy: **Night of the Full Moon** is the strongest choice, as it’s explicitly solo, offline, and free to try [3].

Check the app’s “About this game” section or recent user reviews on Google Play to confirm bot support before downloading.

best offline single player card game And.response:
The best offline single-player card game for Android is **Card Thief**, a strategic solitaire-style game with a dark, immersive theme and no multiplayer requirements [1].

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
**Hanabi Tracker** (Google Play): Utility app for tracking clues and hints while playing the physical Hanabi board game, where players hold cards facing outward so they can't see their own but others can give information. 

Hanabi is the classic cooperative card game matching your description exactly (players must play cards in sequence for fireworks without seeing their own hands). Full digital versions are limited on Android; this tracker enhances physical play, and online options like Board Game Arena exist via browser.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
**Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros.) – The top Android app matching your description. Hold your phone to your forehead like a card/headband; teammates give clues while you guess words from themed decks (celebrities, movies, animals, etc.). Tilt to answer or pass.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.wb.headsup&hl=en_US)

**Charades & Headbands Game** (or Charades & Headbands: Guess Up) – Another popular forehead-guessing app with 60+ charades decks. Place the phone on your head and guess based on clues, mimes, or acting; tilt up/down to proceed.[[2]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=net.artgamestudio.charadesapp&hl=en_US)

These are the main real apps on the Google Play Store that replicate the "hold card to forehead" mechanic using digital cards/decks. Search them directly in the Play Store.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
**Bluff Card Game Offline** (by oengines or similar on Google Play) – The perfect match: a single-player version of the classic bluffing card game also known as Cheat, BS, or I Doubt It. Play against smart AI opponents, lie about your cards, call bluffs, and get rid of your hand first. Fully offline with no internet required.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.oengines.bluffoffline&hl=en_US)

**Poker Offline** (or similar titles like Poker Zmist) – Texas Hold'em and variants against AI bots. Strong emphasis on bluffing, betting strategy, and reading opponents in single-player offline modes.[[2]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.byte3d.poker&hl=en_US)

These are real apps available on the Google Play Store focused on offline single-player bluffing gameplay.
